metadata.description: Prediction tracker for Operation Epic Fury intelligence briefings

predictions.text: Trump says four weeks. I'll believe it when I see week two.
predictions.resolution: Leavitt gave official 4-6 week timeline on Mar 6. Politico sources say war may last until September. Campaign continues at Day 7.

predictions.text: UK trajectory is toward full involvement. 'Specific and limited defensive purpose' historically precedes much broader engagement.
predictions.resolution: UK confirmed bases used for US bomber runs, deployed 4 Typhoons to Qatar, Wildcat helicopters to Cyprus. F-35s scored first combat kills. Full combatant in all but name by Day 5.

predictions.text: Gandhi Hospital is about to become the dominant image of this conflict internationally.
predictions.resolution: Gandhi Hospital neonatal ward destruction was widely reported, but Minab school (175 children) became the dominant image instead. Hospital coverage was significant but superseded.

predictions.text: Every 3-4 hours, the conflict adds a new category of target. The escalation ladder is being climbed from both sides simultaneously, with no visible off-ramp.
predictions.resolution: Pattern held: military sites → leadership → ports → hospitals → nuclear facilities → cultural heritage → schools → commercial shipping → underground bunkers. New target categories every cycle through Day 7.

predictions.text: Expect a COBRA meeting within hours and a Parliamentary statement Monday [after RAF Akrotiri drone strike].
predictions.resolution: UK escalated rapidly — E3 joint statement Mar 3, UK confirmed full base usage for bomber runs.

predictions.text: If Houthis launch and Bab el-Mandeb joins Hormuz and Suez as a disrupted chokepoint, $100+ becomes the floor, not the ceiling.
predictions.resolution: Houthis announced Red Sea attack resumption Mar 3. Red Sea 'effectively closed' to Israeli/US shipping. WTI hit $92 by Mar 6, approaching but not yet at $100. Triple chokepoint partially realized.

predictions.text: Iran's massive barrages may be a 'use it or lose it' strategy — fire everything before the launch sites get hit.
predictions.resolution: IDF confirmed Iran went from 3,000 to <2,000 missiles by Day 4. Launch rate dropped 86% from Day 1. Strategy shifted to 'war of attrition' with fewer, heavier strikes.

predictions.text: The diplomatic off-ramp that Trump announced 12 hours ago is gone [after Larijani denial].
predictions.resolution: Larijani was privately proposing negotiations via Oman even while publicly denying talks. Iran contacted CIA on Day 2. Multiple diplomatic channels remained active despite public rhetoric.

predictions.text: Oil markets are doing something unexpected: not panicking as predicted. Either the market is pricing in a short war, or it's pricing in Hormuz being less closed than announced.
predictions.resolution: Oil initially restrained ($78-82 range) but spiked to $92 by Day 7. Hormuz is selectively closed (Chinese ships passing). Market delayed panic rather than avoided it.

predictions.text: The think tank consensus — that aerial bombing has a 'terrible record at fomenting regime change' — is being proved correct in real time.
The Austin shooting will dominate 48 hours of coverage regardless of whether it's Iran-directed.
predictions.resolution: Austin shooting received significant coverage but was overshadowed by military developments. FBI determination still pending as of Day 7.

predictions.text: Nobody revolts when they're being bombed by foreigners. [Rally-round-the-flag makes regime change harder]
predictions.resolution: Tehran mood initially rallied around government. By Day 6, BBC eyewitness: 'joy of first days is gone.' Kallas says ME countries fear civil war. Pattern shifting but no uprising.

predictions.text: The Israeli assessment that missile attacks will taper is the only genuine de-escalation signal I've seen.
predictions.resolution: Missile launches down 86%, drone launches down 73% from Day 1 by Day 7. 11-hour lull on Day 7. Iran shifted to 'war of attrition' strategy with reduced fire.

predictions.text: E3 produces limited support (ISR, air defense) but no independent strike operations — France may be the exception.
predictions.resolution: UK deployed Typhoons + helicopters, used bases for bomber runs. France deployed Charles de Gaulle carrier. Germany sent warship. No independent E3 strike operations confirmed — support role only.

predictions.text: Larijani back-channel produces ceasefire framework within 72-96 hours if missile exhaustion forces Iran's hand.
predictions.resolution: No ceasefire framework produced. Witkoff cut all diplomatic contacts. Trump declared 'too late for talks' then demanded 'unconditional surrender.' Back-channel did not produce results within timeframe.

predictions.text: Houthis move within 48 hours — triggered by either a major Iranian city hit or fear of being excluded from negotiations.
predictions.resolution: Houthis announced resumption of Red Sea attacks on Mar 3 (same day). Red Sea 'effectively closed' to US/Israeli shipping. But zero confirmed kinetic action against new targets as of Day 7. BBC Arabic: 'Iran didn't ask for help.'

predictions.text: The embassy attack triggers a named escalation within 12-24 hours. The Senate war powers vote fails, probably 45-55 or similar.
predictions.resolution: Senate war powers vote failed 47-53 (close to predicted 45-55). Phase 2 escalation announced. Embassy attack used for political justification but not explicitly named as trigger.

predictions.text: If pro-Iran unrest spreads [in Bahrain], you don't have a war between states anymore — you have internal destabilization of a country hosting critical US military infrastructure.
predictions.resolution: Pro-Iran clashes erupted on Sitra Island but did not spread significantly beyond initial reports. Fifth Fleet operations continued. Thread being monitored.

predictions.text: Wars expand until the cost of continuing exceeds the cost of talking. The Larijani back-channel says Iran is doing that math right now.
predictions.resolution: War continues to expand (Kurdish front, European coalition, Lebanon). Pezeshkian acknowledged mediation on Day 7 — first public crack. Math still being calculated.

predictions.text: Tuesday classified briefings won't shift Republican votes. The real congressional fight is moving to funding.
predictions.resolution: Senate voted 47-53, House 212-219 — both failed. No Republican shift. CSIS flagged $3.5B unbudgeted costs. Funding fight emerging.

predictions.text: NATO fracture is accelerating faster than I expected. The alliance is simultaneously going to war together and tearing itself apart.
predictions.resolution: Germany's Merz distancing from Trump. Spain: 'This war is an error.' Trump threatened trade war with Spain. Italy wasn't told. France deployed carrier. NATO simultaneously at war and fracturing.

predictions.text: The Kurdish front is about to open. Whether Kurdish groups can actually threaten Tehran's hold on power is a different question entirely.
predictions.resolution: Kurdish leaders confirmed coordination with US/Israel to Reuters/AJ on Mar 6. 5,000-6,000 fighters. Ground operation 'very likely within a week.' Front opening but not yet active.

predictions.text: Hormuz is functionally closed. This is the functional closure of the world's most important energy chokepoint.
predictions.resolution: Hormuz transits collapsed from 50+/day to 2/day. P&I insurance cancelled. But closure is SELECTIVE — Chinese ships passing. Not fully closed, but functionally closed for Western shipping.

predictions.text: House vote fails, probably wider margin than Senate 47-53.
predictions.resolution: House defeated war powers resolution 212-219 (7-vote margin vs Senate's 6-vote margin). Wider margin confirmed.

predictions.text: Gulf food crisis dominates humanitarian coverage within 72 hours.
predictions.resolution: Maersk suspended all Gulf cargo bookings. 50M+ affected. Story emerging but not yet dominant — overshadowed by military developments and Minab school.

predictions.text: Oil stays below $108 unless a second commercial vessel is confirmed struck.
predictions.resolution: WTI at $92 on Day 7. Iran struck 'US-owned' oil tanker off Kuwait (second commercial vessel). Qatar warns $150 in 2-3 weeks. Threshold approaching.

predictions.text: The war-duration shift from 'weeks' to 'possibly September' is the quiet escalation markets haven't absorbed. Brent at $84 does not price in six months.
predictions.resolution: WTI spiked to $92 on Day 7. Markets beginning to price in longer conflict. Official timeline now 4-6 weeks. Trump ordered quadruple weapons production — suggesting longer campaign.

predictions.text: The Minab acknowledgment will force a US domestic reckoning within 48-72 hours. Congress will demand hearings. The antiwar movement will find its catalyst.
predictions.resolution: Minab acknowledged on Day 7. 48-72 hour window still active. Both war powers votes already failed. Watching for congressional hearings and domestic protest response.

predictions.text: Trump is genuinely undecided — the 4-6 week window is a hedge: if it's going well by mid-April, declare victory. If not, he's already set the logistics for prolonged campaign.
The Russia intelligence-sharing report from Haaretz is the single most consequential claim this cycle. If true, every casualty from here carries different geopolitical weight.
predictions.resolution: Report remains REPORTED/unconfirmed. Story still only in Hebrew media. Not yet broken into English-language coverage.

predictions.text: The stranded Americans story is going to become a political earthquake. Someone will provide that [dramatic] image. When they do, the narrative shifts.
predictions.resolution: 100K-200K stranded. ~20K returned. State Dept criticized. Story building but no single catalytic image yet.

predictions.text: Oil could reach $100/bbl.
predictions.resolution: WTI at $92 on Day 7. Trajectory heading toward $100. Qatar warns $150 in 2-3 weeks.

predictions.text: European natural gas could jump 130% if Hormuz shipping is halted for one month.
predictions.resolution: European gas prices nearly doubled (€32.50 → €55.70/MWh, ~72% increase) by Day 5 — one week in. 130% not reached yet but trajectory consistent.

predictions.text: Skeptical of regime change prospects through aerial bombing alone.
predictions.resolution: No regime change achieved by Day 7. Succession proceeding under fire. Kurdish proxy forces being organized as alternative approach.

predictions.text: Oil will reach $150/bbl in 2-3 weeks if Hormuz stays closed.
predictions.resolution: WTI at $92 on Day 7. Hormuz selectively closed. 2-3 week window still active.

predictions.text: War may last until September, not weeks.
predictions.resolution: Official timeline: 4-6 weeks. But Trump ordered quadrupled weapons production. CSIS: $891M/day burn rate.

predictions.text: War costs $3.7 billion per 100 hours ($891M/day), with $3.5B already unbudgeted.
predictions.resolution: CSIS analysis published and widely cited.

predictions.text: Middle East countries privately fear civil war in Iran.
predictions.resolution: BBC: Tehran mood shifting. Internet blackout ongoing. No civil war signs yet, but social cohesion deteriorating.

predictions.text: Strait of Hormuz will reopen selectively within 2-3 weeks, fully within 4-6 weeks, contingent on ceasefire or Iranian military exhaustion.
No US/Israeli ground invasion of Iran proper. Kurdish proxy operations will be the 'ground' component.
predictions.resolution: Hegseth: ground troops 'not part of the plan at this time.' Kurdish ground op 'very likely within a week.'

predictions.text: Conflict will expand to include direct Saudi military action against Iran (beyond interception) within 2 weeks.
predictions.resolution: Saudi Arabia intercepting drones/missiles. Ras Tanura struck. Multiple reports of Arab countries considering joining attacks.

predictions.text: A ceasefire or de-escalation agreement will be reached before the 6-week mark (mid-April), driven by Iranian missile exhaustion and oil price pressure on the US.
predictions.resolution: Pezeshkian acknowledged mediation. Missiles down 86%. But Trump demands 'unconditional surrender.'

predictions.text: WTI crude will exceed $100/bbl within the next 7 days.
predictions.resolution: WTI at $92 on Mar 6. Qatar warns $150 in 2-3 weeks. Commercial shipping under attack.

predictions.text: The Iranian government will survive the current conflict — no regime collapse or successful coup during active hostilities.
predictions.resolution: Interim Leadership Council functioning. Mojtaba named successor. Government fragmented but operating.

predictions.text: Kurdish ground operations will begin within 10 days but will not meaningfully threaten Tehran's control over core Persian provinces.
predictions.resolution: 5,000-6,000 fighters. Ground op 'very likely within a week' per Kurdish leaders.

predictions.text: The war will become a major factor in 2026 US midterm elections, with approval ratings remaining below 35%.
predictions.resolution: Current approval: 25-27%. Both war powers votes failed. $891M/day cost. 100K+ stranded Americans.

predictions.text: Houthis will conduct a significant kinetic attack on a US/Israeli-linked vessel or military target within 5 days.
predictions.resolution: Houthis declared but zero kinetic action confirmed. Red Sea 'effectively closed.' USS Ford entered Red Sea.

predictions.text: Iran's internet blackout will be partially restored within 48 hours, triggering domestic information shock and potential unrest.
predictions.resolution: Internet blackout at 144+ hours. 88 million people without information access.
